settings.hub.local_storage.remember_prom: Remember the last prompt and parameters
Write prompt and parameter state locally so the last-used prompts and parameters are restored on the next launch.
settings.hub.local_storage.delete_behavi: Controls whether image deletions from generation preview, history, and similar actions go to the system Recycle Bin or are permanently deleted.
Generated images are no longer automatically saved to the Output folder or history. Save them manually to keep them.
settings.hub.local_storage.strip_metadat: Automatically strips metadata from any saved image, even when using non-stripped save options. Images saved before enabling this are not affected.
settings.hub.local_storage.auto_copy_vib: After saving a Vibe pre-encode cache, archives verified source images into vibe/origin and records them with relative paths.

settings.hub.performance.unload_after_in: Unload ONNX models from VRAM/memory after inference
Applies to all ONNX models, including upscaling and reverse tag/tag inference. Turn this off to keep models loaded and reduce reload time. Recommended for users on mechanical hard drives.

about.description:
This tool is completely free. If you paid to obtain this tool, please report the seller.

Under certain settings, generation requests may consume Anlas from an Opus subscription account. Such operations are highlighted in yellow. Anlas is calculated and deducted by NovelAI, and is unrelated to this tool or its author.

This tool was designed around the workflows of Opus subscribers. Please note that users on lower tiers, or with expired subscriptions, will have Anlas deducted for all generation requests.

This tool connects only to the official NovelAI API and does not collect, upload, or store user data for the author or any third party.

Your API key is encrypted using Windows DPAPI and stored in user/config/apiconfig.json. It is bound to your Windows user account and this machine, and cannot be decrypted elsewhere.
about.thanks.dominik_reh: Author of the tag auto-complete feature
about.thanks.header: The following projects, authors, and teams directly or indirectly supported features in this tool:
about.thanks.jiarandiana0307: Source provider for the image obfuscation algorithm
about.thanks.pixai_labs: Author of the pixai-tagger reverse model
about.thanks.smilingwolf: Author of the WD14 tagger model and methodology
about.thanks.novelai: Official / PNG steganographic data code reference
help.highlights.actions.body:
Users can quickly insert interaction prefixes from the context menu action helpers in both the main prompt and character prompt.

The initiator uses source#, the passive side uses target#, and mutual interactions use mutual#. If nothing is selected, the prefix is inserted at the current caret position. This matches the standard format used in post-NAI4 versions and is helpful for character interactions, gaze relationships, and action direction.
help.highlights.actions.title: Action Interactions
help.highlights.automation.body:
Supports standard one-off generation as well as automated continuous runs.

You can lock in a base prompt and parameters, then let the tool send requests in a loop and combine that with random style prompt features to produce many variations for style tests, composition tests, and inspiration sweeps.
help.highlights.automation.title: Automated Generation
help.highlights.large_inpaint.body:
The tool uses a local crop-based approach for large-image inpainting, keeping the edited area inside a practical generation range whenever possible.

That makes it possible to refine large artworks more precisely and at lower cost without repainting the entire enlarged image.
help.highlights.large_inpaint.title: Large Image Inpaint
help.highlights.post.body:
Includes a post-processing workspace for light touch-up after generation.

It is useful for style unification, quick visual enhancement, and rapid effect testing without constantly switching to external software.
help.highlights.post.title: Post Filters
help.highlights.prompt_generator.body:
The tools menu includes a prompt generator that turns image descriptions, characters, scenes, or loose ideas into English prompts suitable for NovelAI image generation.

It supports Booru tags, Booru tags with natural language, and natural-language output. Results can be sent straight to the current prompt box or copied for further editing.
help.highlights.prompt_generator.title: Prompt Generation
help.highlights.prompt_normalization.bod:
Built-in prompt normalization helps unify tag style, clean up formatting, and prevent prompts from getting messier over time.

Over long-term use, this reduces repetitive editing and makes it easier to turn prompts from different sources into stable reusable formats.
help.highlights.quick_prompts.body:
You can maintain your own quick-prompt library and insert saved fragments whenever needed.

Character templates, quality tags, camera language, and common negative tags can all become part of your personal workflow.
help.highlights.quick_prompts.title: Quick Prompts
help.highlights.random_style.body:
The tool can sample random style prompts from the built-in tag sheet to help you explore visual directions quickly.

This is especially efficient when you do not have a fixed target style or want one theme to branch into many aesthetic variations.
help.highlights.random_style.title: Random Style Prompts
help.highlights.inspect.body:
Supports inspecting common parameters and prompt metadata from NovelAI and Stable Diffusion images.

This lets you review settings from existing images, reproduce results quickly, continue editing, or send those parameters back into the current workflow.
help.highlights.inspect.title: Inspect NAI / SD Images
help.highlights.reverse.body:
When an image has no usable generation metadata, the tool can call an external reverse-tagger model to infer tags from the image contents.

Results are written back into the inspect workspace, making it easier to continue in image generation mode, with optional character tags, copyright tags, and threshold control.
help.highlights.reverse.title: Reverse Tagging
help.highlights.upscale.body:
Includes a local upscale workspace that uses ONNX models such as Real-ESRGAN Anime 6B to enlarge images locally.

It supports multiple scale options, works offline, and does not consume Anlas. After processing, the preview auto-fits, and the result can be sent to other workspaces for follow-up edits.
help.highlights.upscale.title: Local Upscale
help.highlights.weight_converter.body:
The tools menu includes a dedicated weight converter that can convert between Stable Diffusion weights, NovelAI 1-3 classic bracket weights, and NovelAI 4+ numeric double-colon weights.

The side-by-side layout makes it easy to preview input and output before sending the result back into the current workspace, especially when cleaning old prompts or moving between ecosystems.
help.highlights.weight_converter.title: Weight Conversion
help.highlights.wildcards.body:
You can organize reusable variable prompt fragments as txt entries under user/wildcards/ and expand them automatically by seed before sending.

They work with auto-complete, support variable reuse and weight syntax, and are well suited for clothing, hair color, composition elements, and style fragments.
help.highlights.wildcards.title: Prompt Wildcards

help.overview.image.body:
1. Select the model, enter prompts, and adjust parameters in the left panel.
2. Click Generate or press Ctrl+Enter to send the request.
3. Results are saved automatically to the output/ folder.
4. You can continue by sending the result to I2I or post-processing.
help.overview.image.title: Image Generation
help.overview.i2i.body:
1. Import an image, then choose Inpaint for masked edits or Denoise for whole-image variation.
2. Adjust the mask, denoise strength, noise, prompts, and size as needed.
3. Click Generate to send the I2I request; apply, compare, redo, or discard the result from the result bar.
help.overview.i2i.title: I2I Mode
help.overview.shortcuts.body:
Ctrl+Z / Ctrl+Y: Undo / Redo mask
Ctrl+I: Invert mask
Ctrl+Shift+I: Fill blank area
Ctrl+D: Clear mask
Ctrl++: Expand mask
Ctrl+-: Shrink mask
Ctrl+Enter: Send generation request
Ctrl+S: Save
Ctrl+Shift+S: Save as
help.overview.shortcuts.title: Shortcuts
help.overview.tools.body:
B: Brush
E: Eraser
R: Rectangle selection
Mouse wheel: Adjust brush size
help.overview.tools.title: Drawing Tools
help.overview.view.body:
Middle mouse drag: Pan viewport
Ctrl+Wheel: Zoom viewport
Right drag / Alt+Left drag: Move base image
A+Arrow keys: Align image
A+Num0: Center image
help.overview.view.title: View Controls
help.overview.wildcards.body:
1. `__name__`: draw one item
2. `__folder/name__`: draw from a subfolder entry
3. `__name@3__`: draw 3 items and join them with commas
4. `__name@var__`: draw one item and save it to variable var
5. `__@var__`: reuse a variable already drawn in the current request
6. When a .txt line is written as `content|1.2`, it is converted to the current model's weight syntax
7. If double-underscore triggering is disabled in Usage Settings, the raw entry name can trigger a wildcard directly
help.overview.wildcards.title: Wildcard Syntax

oobe.api.title: API Settings
oobe.api.description: Enter your API Key in this box. Keep it private and do not share it with others.
oobe.api.find_key_steps:
1. Open the official NovelAI image generation page
2. Find and click the icon that looks like three horizontal lines
3. Choose Account Settings
4. Find Persistent API Token and click it
5. Copy the token and paste it into the box
oobe.api.test.empty: Enter your API Key in this box. Keep it private and do not share it with others.
oobe.api.test.valid: API Key is valid. Store it carefully and avoid leaking it.
oobe.api.test.invalid: API Key is invalid or the network is unavailable.
oobe.asset_protection.title: Enable Asset Protection Mode
oobe.asset_protection.description: Helps prevent accidental Anlas spending. When enabled, it restricts advanced features such as vibe transfer and precise reference, plus high-resolution image generation. You can change this later in Quota Management.
oobe.api.skip_hint: You can leave this empty, but image generation and inpainting services will not be available.

oobe.done.description: Setup is complete. You can start using NovelAI Utility Tool.
